title: Run Gemma on the edge with the Coral Board
summary: Google's Coral Board is a low-power development board featuring the Coral NPU machine learning accelerator for running Gemma models on edge devices. The board ships with integrated I/O including a screen, camera, microphones, and LEDs, with demonstrated use cases including live translation, natural language control of hardware, and multimodal applications like music generation.
writeup: The video demonstrates running Gemma on Google's Coral Board, a low-power development board with an embedded Coral NPU accelerator built for on-device AI. It matters because it moves model inference off the cloud and onto the edge, where local execution can run without a network connection. Key takeaways: the board targets edge AI experimentation, and it ships with integrated peripherals including a screen, camera, microphones, and LEDs for building and evaluating on-device models.
